comment_text.ud5.3:4.3: It’s interesting to note that, despite the perhaps derogatory sense of kuṭṭhi (“leper”), there is no question that Suppabuddha sits in the assembly and is not physically exlcuded.
comment_text.ud5.3:11.5: At Taittirīya Brāhmaṇa 3.2.9b, a titan renders the sacrificial ground impure by spitting on it.

root_text.ud5.3:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.ud5.3:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena rājagahe suppabuddho nāma kuṭṭhī ahosi—
root_text.ud5.3:1.5: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā mahatiyā parisāya parivuto dhammaṁ desento nisinno hoti.
root_text.ud5.3:2.1: Addasā kho suppabuddho kuṭṭhī taṁ mahājanakāyaṁ dūratova sannipatitaṁ.
root_text.ud5.3:2.3: “nissaṁsayaṁ kho ettha kiñci khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā bhājīyati.
root_text.ud5.3:2.5: Appeva nāmettha kiñci khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā labheyyan”ti.
root_text.ud5.3:3.1: Atha kho suppabuddho kuṭṭhī yena so mahājanakāyo tenupasaṅkami.
root_text.ud5.3:3.2: Addasā kho suppabuddho kuṭṭhī bhagavantaṁ mahatiyā parisāya parivutaṁ dhammaṁ desentaṁ nisinnaṁ.
root_text.ud5.3:3.4: “na kho ettha kiñci khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā bhājīyati.
root_text.ud5.3:3.5: Samaṇo ayaṁ gotamo parisati dhammaṁ deseti.
root_text.ud5.3:4.1: Atha kho bhagavā sabbāvantaṁ parisaṁ cetasā ceto paricca manasākāsi
root_text.ud5.3:4.2: “ko nu kho idha bhabbo dhammaṁ viññātun”ti?
root_text.ud5.3:4.3: Addasā kho bhagavā suppabuddhaṁ kuṭṭhiṁ tassaṁ parisāyaṁ nisinnaṁ.
root_text.ud5.3:4.6: Suppabuddhaṁ kuṭṭhiṁ ārabbha anupubbiṁ kathaṁ kathesi, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.ud5.3:4.7: dānakathaṁ sīlakathaṁ saggakathaṁ; kāmānaṁ ādīnavaṁ okāraṁ saṅkilesaṁ; nekkhamme ānisaṁsaṁ pakāsesi.
root_text.ud5.3:4.8: Yadā bhagavā aññāsi suppabuddhaṁ kuṭṭhiṁ kallacittaṁ muducittaṁ vinīvaraṇacittaṁ udaggacittaṁ pasannacittaṁ, atha yā buddhānaṁ sāmukkaṁsikā dhammadesanā taṁ pakāsesi—
root_text.ud5.3:4.10: Seyyathāpi nāma suddhaṁ vatthaṁ apagatakāḷakaṁ sammadeva rajanaṁ paṭiggaṇheyya;
root_text.ud5.3:4.11: evamevaṁ suppabuddhassa kuṭṭhissa tasmiṁyeva āsane virajaṁ vītamalaṁ dhammacakkhuṁ udapādi:
root_text.ud5.3:5.1: Atha kho suppabuddho kuṭṭhī diṭṭhadhammo pattadhammo viditadhammo pariyogāḷhadhammo tiṇṇavicikiccho vigatakathaṅkatho vesārajjappatto aparappaccayo satthu sāsane uṭṭhāyāsanā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho suppabuddho kuṭṭhī bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud5.3:6.2: Seyyathāpi, bhante, nikkujjitaṁ vā ukkujjeyya, paṭicchannaṁ vā vivareyya, mūḷhassa vā maggaṁ ācikkheyya, andhakāre vā telapajjotaṁ dhāreyya: ‘cakkhumanto rūpāni dakkhantī’ti; evamevaṁ bhagavatā anekapariyāyena dhammo pakāsito.
root_text.ud5.3:6.3: Esāhaṁ, bhante, bhagavantaṁ saraṇaṁ gacchāmi dhammañca bhikkhusaṅghañca.
root_text.ud5.3:6.4: Upāsakaṁ maṁ bhagavā dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.
root_text.ud5.3:7.1: Atha kho suppabuddho kuṭṭhī bhagavatā dhammiyā kathāya sandassito samādapito samuttejito sampahaṁsito bhagavatā bhāsitaṁ abhinanditvā anumoditvā uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi.
root_text.ud5.3:7.2: Atha kho acirapakkantaṁ suppabuddhaṁ kuṭṭhiṁ gāvī taruṇavacchā adhipatitvā jīvitā voropesi.
root_text.ud5.3:8.1: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu.
root_text.ud5.3:8.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.ud5.3:8.3: “yo so, bhante, suppabuddho nāma kuṭṭhī bhagavatā dhammiyā kathāya sandassito samādapito samuttejito sampahaṁsito, so kālaṅkato.
root_text.ud5.3:9.1: “Paṇḍito, bhikkhave, suppabuddho kuṭṭhī; paccapādi dhammassānudhammaṁ; na ca maṁ dhammādhikaraṇaṁ vihesesi.
root_text.ud5.3:9.2: Suppabuddho, bhikkhave, kuṭṭhī tiṇṇaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā sotāpanno avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyaṇo”ti.
root_text.ud5.3:10.1: Evaṁ vutte, aññataro bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud5.3:10.2: “ko nu kho, bhante, hetu, ko paccayo yena suppabuddho kuṭṭhī ahosi—
root_text.ud5.3:11.1: “Bhūtapubbaṁ, bhikkhave, suppabuddho kuṭṭhī imasmiṁyeva rājagahe seṭṭhiputto ahosi.
root_text.ud5.3:11.2: So uyyānabhūmiṁ niyyanto addasa tagarasikhiṁ paccekabuddhaṁ nagaraṁ piṇḍāya pavisantaṁ.
root_text.ud5.3:11.6: So tassa kammassa vipākena bahūni vassasatāni bahūni vassasahassāni bahūni vassasatasahassāni niraye paccittha.
root_text.ud5.3:11.7: Tasseva kammassa vipākāvasesena imasmiṁyeva rājagahe kuṭṭhī ahosi manussadaliddo, manussakapaṇo, manussavarāko.
root_text.ud5.3:11.8: So tathāgatappaveditaṁ dhammavinayaṁ āgamma saddhaṁ samādiyi sīlaṁ samādiyi sutaṁ samādiyi cāgaṁ samādiyi paññaṁ samādiyi.
root_text.ud5.3:11.9: So tathāgatappaveditaṁ dhammavinayaṁ āgamma saddhaṁ samādiyitvā sīlaṁ samādiyitvā sutaṁ samādiyitvā cāgaṁ samādiyitvā paññaṁ samādiyitvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapanno devānaṁ tāvatiṁsānaṁ sahabyataṁ.
root_text.ud5.3:11.10: So tattha aññe deve atirocati vaṇṇena ceva yasasā cā”ti.
root_text.ud5.3:12.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud5.3:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.ud5.3:1.3: At that time in Rājagaha there was a leper called Suppabuddha.
translation_text.ud5.3:1.4: He was a poor person, destitute, and pitiful.
translation_text.ud5.3:1.5: Now, at that time the Buddha was teaching Dhamma, surrounded by a large assembly.
translation_text.ud5.3:2.1: Suppabuddha saw the crowd seated off in the distance
translation_text.ud5.3:2.3: “Surely some fresh and cooked foods will be distributed there.
translation_text.ud5.3:2.4: Why don’t I approach the crowd?
translation_text.ud5.3:2.5: Hopefully I’ll get something to eat.”
translation_text.ud5.3:3.2: where he saw the Buddha teaching Dhamma, surrounded by a large assembly.
translation_text.ud5.3:3.3: When he saw this he thought,
translation_text.ud5.3:3.5: The ascetic Gotama is teaching Dhamma in an assembly.
translation_text.ud5.3:3.6: Why don’t I also listen to the teaching?”
translation_text.ud5.3:3.7: Right there he sat down to one side, thinking,
translation_text.ud5.3:3.8: “I also will listen to the teaching.”
translation_text.ud5.3:4.1: Then the Buddha focused on encompassing the minds of everyone in the assembly, thinking,
translation_text.ud5.3:4.2: “Who here is capable of understanding the teaching?”
translation_text.ud5.3:4.3: He saw Suppabuddha sitting in the assembly,
translation_text.ud5.3:4.5: “He is capable of understanding the teaching.”
translation_text.ud5.3:4.6: He gave a step by step talk especially for Suppabuddha
translation_text.ud5.3:4.7: on giving, ethical conduct, and heaven. He explained the drawbacks of sensual pleasures, so sordid and corrupt, and the benefit of renunciation.
translation_text.ud5.3:4.8: When the Buddha knew that Suppabuddha’s mind was ready, supple, without hindrances, elated, and confident, he revealed the teaching unique to the Buddhas:
translation_text.ud5.3:4.9: suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path.
translation_text.ud5.3:4.10: Just as a clean cloth rid of stains would properly absorb dye,
translation_text.ud5.3:4.11: in that very seat the stainless, immaculate vision of the Dhamma arose in Suppabuddha:
translation_text.ud5.3:4.12: “Everything that has a beginning has an end.”
translation_text.ud5.3:5.1: Then Suppabuddha saw, attained, understood, and fathomed the Dhamma. He went beyond doubt, got rid of indecision, and became self-assured and independent of others regarding the Teacher’s instructions. He rose from his seat and went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said:
translation_text.ud5.3:6.2: As if he were righting the overturned, or revealing the hidden, or pointing out the path to the lost, or lighting a lamp in the dark so people with clear eyes can see what’s there, the Buddha has made the teaching clear in many ways.
translation_text.ud5.3:6.3: I go for refuge to the Buddha, to the teaching, and to the mendicant Saṅgha.
translation_text.ud5.3:6.4: From this day forth, may the Buddha remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”
translation_text.ud5.3:7.1: After Suppabuddha had been educated, encouraged, fired up, and inspired with a Dhamma talk by the Buddha, he got up from his seat, bowed, and respectfully circled the Buddha before leaving.
translation_text.ud5.3:7.2: But soon after Suppabuddha had left, a cow with a baby calf charged at him and took his life.
translation_text.ud5.3:8.1: Then several mendicants went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said,
translation_text.ud5.3:8.3: “The leper named Suppabuddha, after being educated, encouraged, fired up, and inspired with a Dhamma talk by the Buddha, has passed away.
translation_text.ud5.3:8.4: Where has he been reborn in his next life?”
translation_text.ud5.3:9.1: “Mendicants, Suppabuddha was astute. He practiced in line with the teaching, and did not trouble me about the teachings.
translation_text.ud5.3:9.2: With the ending of three fetters, Suppabuddha is a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.”
translation_text.ud5.3:10.1: When he said this, one of the mendicants said to the Buddha,
translation_text.ud5.3:10.2: “What is the cause, sir, what is the reason why Suppabuddha became a leper,
translation_text.ud5.3:10.3: a poor person, destitute, and pitiful?”
translation_text.ud5.3:11.1: “Once upon a time, mendicants, Suppabuddha used to be a financier’s son right here in Rājagaha.
translation_text.ud5.3:11.2: On his way to visit a park, he saw Tagarasikhī, an Independent Buddha, entering the city for alms.
translation_text.ud5.3:11.3: When he saw this he thought,
translation_text.ud5.3:11.4: “Who is this leper wandering about with his leper’s robe?”
translation_text.ud5.3:11.5: Before leaving, he spat on the ground and turned his left side to Tagarasikhī.
translation_text.ud5.3:11.6: As a result of that deed he burned in hell for many years, for many hundreds, many thousands, many hundreds of thousands of years.
translation_text.ud5.3:11.7: And as a residual result of that same deed, he became a leper right here in Rājagaha, a poor person, destitute and pitiful.
translation_text.ud5.3:11.8: But owing to faith in the teaching and training proclaimed by the Realized One, he undertook ethics, learning, generosity, and wisdom.
translation_text.ud5.3:11.9: After undertaking these things, when his body broke up, after death, he was reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm, in the company of the gods of the thirty-three.
translation_text.ud5.3:11.10: There he outshines the other gods in beauty and glory.”
translation_text.ud5.3:12.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud5.3:13.1: “As a clear-eyed man would avoid rough paths,
translation_text.ud5.3:13.2: so long as strength is found;
translation_text.ud5.3:13.3: an astute person in the living world,

variant_text.ud5.3:4.6: anupubbiṁ kathaṁ → ānupubbīkathaṁ (bj); anupubbīkathaṁ (si); anupubbikathaṁ (sya-all, pts-vp-pli1, mr)
variant_text.ud5.3:4.7: nekkhamme → nekkhamme ca (bj, sya-all); nikkhame ca (pts-vp-pli1)
variant_text.ud5.3:7.2: acirapakkantaṁ suppabuddhaṁ kuṭṭhiṁ → atha kho suppabuddhaṁ kuṭṭhiṁ acirapakkantaṁ (bj); atha kho suppabuddhaṁ acirapakkantaṁ (si); atha kho suppabuddhaṁ (pts-vp-pli1)
